rules: no generic compliment ('I found your article amazing')

templates.name: Widget Embed Offer
templates.subject_format: Quick tool idea for your [article title]
templates.template:
Hi [Name],

I read your article "[Article Title]" — the section about [specific point] is exactly what people need to hear.

I built a free [mortgage affordability / debt payoff] calculator that lets readers plug in their own numbers instead of just reading a static example. No signup, no data collection — just a clean calculator they can use right on the page.

Would this be useful for your readers? Happy to send the embed code.
templates.notes: Reference a SPECIFIC section/paragraph from their article. The 'instead of just reading a static example' line is the key value prop.

templates.name: Custom Calculation Example Offer
templates.template:
Hi [Name],

Your article "[Article Title]" walks through [specific calculation] — really clear explanation.

I can create an interactive version where your readers enter their own numbers and see their personal result. Same math, but personalized. No signup, completely free.

Would that be useful for the article? Happy to build it.
templates.notes: The key phrase is 'same math, but personalized' — it shows you understand their content.

templates.name: Broken/Outdated Resource Replacement Offer
templates.subject_format: Quick note: broken calculator link on your [page name]
templates.template:
Hi [Name],

I noticed your [resource page / article] at [URL] links to a [calculator/tool] that [is broken / no longer works / isn't mobile-friendly].

I have a working, updated version that does the same thing — free, no signup, mobile-friendly. Your readers would get a working tool instead of a dead link.

Would you like me to send the updated link? Takes 30 seconds to swap.
templates.notes: Lead with the broken link — that's the hook. Site owners hate 404s and broken tools.
